sections.paragraph:
-

(1) subst. m. a pipe, a flute, so called from its being pierced (see the root חָלַל No. 1 ), Isa 5:12 30:29 1Ki 1:40.

(2) adj. profane (see the root Piel No. 3, b, and Hiphil No. 3), and neut. any thing profane, whence with ה parag. חָלִילָה, חָלִלָה (Milêl) properly, to profane things! ad profana, i.e. absit, far be it! (Talmud. חולין לך), an exclamation of abhorrence. 1Sa 20:2, חָלִילָה לֹא חָמוּת “far be it! thou shalt not die;” comp. 1Sa 2:30. It is used-(a) חָלִילָה לִי followed by מִן with an inf. “far be it from me that I should (so) do,” Gen 18:25 44:7 , 17 Gen 44:17 Jos 24:16 comp. Job 34:10.-(b) followed by אִם with a future, Job 27:5 1Sa 14:45 (without לִי) 2Sa 20:20. To both of these expressions there is sometimes added מֵיהֹוָה 1Sa 24:7 26:11 1Ki 21:3, with the sense of, to places profaned or accursed by the Lord (see מִן 2, ); or, the primary signification being neglected, a curse be to me from the Lord, if, etc. Jos 22:29, חָלִילָה לָנוּ מִמֶּנּוּ לִמְרֹד בַּיְהֹוָה “woe be to us from him (i.e. Jehovah), if we should sin against Jehovah.” The idea is a little different, 1Sa 20:9, “far be it from thee, (for me) that if I know I tell thee not.” [“In this passage instead of the dat. of the person detesting, there is added a dat. of the person for whose benefit these things are sworn.”]
